Your Trainer › Manual

Manual & Guides

Last updated: 2026-05-10

Everything you need to get the most out of Your Trainer — beyond what fits in the app itself.

Start here

Getting Started — first-ride walkthrough from install to your first completed workout. About thirty minutes start-to-finish.

Workout library

Bundled training sessions across the main categories — base, sweet spot, threshold, VO2max, HR-Zone, routes — plus how to import community workouts and how the visual editor works. Browse the workout library →

Quick Ride, Free Ride & Zone Ride

The three unstructured-ride modes — one per workout tab. Quick Ride holds a wattage; Free Ride gives you slope and gears; Zone Ride keeps you in a target heart-rate zone via the HR Zone-Lock thermostat. Read the unstructured-rides guide →

Personal Bests & Ghost Rider

How Your Trainer tracks personal bests for each workout (per-domain metrics — heart-rate cost on power, average wattage on HR-Zone, completion time on routes), and how the ghost rider on routes lets you race your previous best. Read about Personal Bests →

Riders & Family

Multi-rider household setup — what's per-rider, the rider card, the rating panel that unlocks after five sessions (PWR, SPR, CLB, END, CON, HRE), family leaderboard, family ghosts on routes. Read the riders guide →

AI prompt skills

Tips, patterns, and tested example prompts for the AI Workout Coach. Plus the Ask-AI-Coach input on the Workout History screen, for plain-language questions about your training. Read the AI guide →

Advanced workout authoring

The full .ytw workout file format. Build workouts in any text editor, import via the share sheet, share with friends. See the workout schema →

Troubleshooting

Common issues across connection, ride feel, imports, sync, AI Coach, and language — with the fix or workaround for each. Browse troubleshooting →

Reference

  • Glossary — cycling, training, and hardware terms used across the manual, defined in plain language.
  • Training Zones — power and HR zones explained, including the Coggan-7 mapping and how to find your FTP and LTHR.
  • Hardware Guide — supported smart trainers, HRMs, device recommendations (tablets and phones), mounting suggestions.
  • Routes Guide — slope-driven simulation rides: where to find route files, the elevation requirement, and how to enrich a flat file.
  • FAQ — privacy, compatibility, cost, features, and support questions.
  • Safety & Fitness — indoor-cycling safety, hydration, mechanical checks, when to back off.
  • Release Notes — what's new in each Your Trainer release.

If something specific would help you that isn't here, drop us a note at support@your-applications.com.
